source_pipeline: configs/countries/us.yaml → sync_socrata.py + sync_census_popest.py → raw.us_sf_* → dbt_us_staging → dbt_us_analytics → dbt_us_marts → export_us_sf.py
perimeter: Adopted budget (Annual Appropriation Ordinance), ALL funds, citywide, NET of the dataset's embedded negative 'Transfer Adjustment' rows — those lines are carried explicitly (never silently netted) as transfer_adjustment_usd at every altitude and flagged in the dept × character cells.

spending.label: Salaries
spending.gloss: Pay for the City's workforce — police officers, nurses, Muni operators, librarians and everyone else on the payroll.

spending.label: Non-Personnel Services
spending.gloss: Services the City buys rather than staffs: professional services, maintenance contracts, utilities, rent, training, travel.

spending.label: Mandatory Fringe Benefits
spending.gloss: The employer's share of employee benefits the City is required to fund: pension contributions, health coverage, Social Security and Medicare.

spending.label: Intrafund Transfers Out
spending.gloss: Accounting transfers between accounts inside the same fund — internal money movement, not services delivered. Cancelled citywide by the transfer adjustment.

spending.label: Debt Service
spending.gloss: Principal and interest payments on bonds and other borrowing.

spending.label: Services Of Other Depts
spending.gloss: Internal billing: one City department paying another for its work (legal counsel, fleet, IT, real estate). Money that stays inside the City.

spending.label: Transfers Out
spending.gloss: Money moved from one City fund to another (for example General Fund support to Muni). Cancelled citywide by the transfer adjustment.

spending.label: City Grant Program
spending.gloss: Grants the City makes to outside organizations — mostly nonprofits delivering health, housing and social services.

spending.label: Capital Outlay
spending.gloss: Purchases of physical assets: equipment, vehicles, building improvements, infrastructure.

spending.label: Materials & Supplies
spending.gloss: Physical goods the City consumes: pharmaceuticals, fuel, food, office and maintenance supplies.

spending.label: Aid Payments
spending.gloss: Direct assistance paid to or on behalf of individuals — public-assistance grants such as CalWORKs and county adult assistance.

spending.label: Programmatic Projects
spending.gloss: Budget set aside for defined projects and initiatives, spent through the programs that carry them out.

spending.label: Unappropriated Rev Retained
spending.gloss: Revenue budgeted but deliberately not appropriated for spending — retained as fund balance or reserve.

spending.label: Unappropriated Rev-Designated
spending.gloss: Revenue set aside for a designated future purpose rather than appropriated for spending this year.

spending.label: Other Support/Care of Persons
spending.gloss: Care purchased for people the City is responsible for: foster care, board-and-care, shelter beds, treatment placements.

spending.label: Facilities Maintenance
spending.gloss: Upkeep of City buildings and facilities: repairs, renovation and maintenance projects.

spending.label: Aid Assistance
spending.gloss: In-kind and support assistance tied to public-aid programs, alongside direct aid payments.

spending.label: Carry-Forward Budgets Only
spending.gloss: Budget authority carried forward from a prior year — a budgeting entry, not new money.

spending.label: Overhead and Allocations
spending.gloss: Overhead charged out to other departments and funds; appears negative where a department recovers its overhead costs from the departments it serves.

spending.label: Transfer Adjustment - Uses
spending.gloss: The negative adjustment that cancels inter- and intra-fund transfers so the citywide total counts each dollar once.

revenue.label: Charges for Services
revenue.gloss: Fees charged for services — dominated by the City's enterprises billing their customers: hospital care billed to insurers and Medi-Cal, airport charges to airlines, water and sewer bills. Not primarily fees on residents.

revenue.label: Property Taxes
revenue.gloss: The City's share of the 1% property tax on assessed value, plus voter-approved add-ons.

revenue.label: IntraFund Transfers In
revenue.gloss: Accounting transfers between accounts inside the same fund — internal mechanics, not money coming in from outside.

revenue.label: Other Local Taxes
revenue.gloss: Local taxes other than property and business taxes: the City's sales-tax share, hotel tax, real-estate transfer tax, parking tax, utility users tax.

revenue.label: Intergovernmental: State
revenue.gloss: Money from the State of California: health and social-services realignment funds and program reimbursements.

revenue.label: Expenditure Recovery
revenue.gloss: One department recovering its costs from another — internal billing income, the mirror of 'Services of Other Depts' on the spending side.

revenue.label: Business Taxes
revenue.gloss: Taxes on business activity: gross receipts tax, homelessness gross receipts tax, business registration fees.

revenue.label: Transfers In
revenue.gloss: Money received from another City fund — the receiving side of fund-to-fund transfers. Cancelled citywide by the transfer adjustment.

revenue.label: Rents & Concessions
revenue.gloss: Rent and concession income from City property: airport concessions, port leases, facility rentals.

revenue.label: Intergovernmental: Federal
revenue.gloss: Federal money: Medicaid and Medicare reimbursements plus federal grants for housing, transit and emergencies.

revenue.label: Unappropriated Fund Balance
revenue.gloss: Prior-year fund balance brought into the budget as a source of funding.

revenue.label: Other Revenues
revenue.gloss: Revenue that fits no other category — settlements, donations, miscellaneous income.

revenue.label: Intergovernmental: Other
revenue.gloss: Money from other governments — regional agencies, other counties and special districts.

revenue.label: Fines, Forfeiture, & Penalties
revenue.gloss: Parking and traffic fines, court penalties, code-enforcement fines.

revenue.label: Contributions Ret/HSS/HlthCare
revenue.gloss: Employee and employer contributions flowing into the retirement and health-benefit systems.

revenue.label: Interest & Investment Income
revenue.gloss: Earnings on the City's cash and investment pools.

revenue.label: Licenses, Permits,& Franchises
revenue.gloss: Business licenses, construction and use permits, franchise fees from utilities.

revenue.label: Other Financing Sources
revenue.gloss: Financing inflows: bond and loan proceeds, proceeds from asset sales.

revenue.label: Transfer Adjustment-Source
revenue.gloss: The negative adjustment on the revenue side that cancels inter- and intra-fund transfers so the citywide total counts each dollar once.

program_strip.note: From FY2019 the source's program dimension is ~10 generic activity tags (Operating / Capital / Administrative…) — an operating-vs-capital strip, not a drill level.

notes: display_name and gloss fields are a provenance-flagged IN-SESSION enrichment layer (seed_us_sf_dept_names, seed_us_sf_character_glosses, 2026-07-16) — source labels are always carried verbatim alongside and never overwritten. Departments with a null display_name (legacy years) render their source label.
